danger_signals.visual_form: Cows, deer, cars, and trees rendered on track
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazards that cause motorcycle ejection and damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Stamina meter in bottom-left corner decreasing during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability to ejection from bike; combat pressure indicator

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy stamina meter displayed bottom-right corner
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent proximity and combat readiness state

danger_signals.visual_form: Damage meter depleting with each crash
danger_signals.meaning: Bike structural integrity declining; total wreckage imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Police figures appearing sporadically on tracks
danger_signals.meaning: Enforcement threat; apprehension results in fine penalty and race termination

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual sense of reduced velocity after crashes or combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear road visible with no obstacles or opponents immediately proximate
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to accelerate and gain position without immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Prize money awarded and displayed after race completion
safety_signals.meaning: Resources available for bike upgrades and repair/fine coverage

safety_signals.visual_form: Race end screen with password generation
safety_signals.meaning: Progress secured; advancement to next difficulty tier unlocked

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent motorcyclists rendered with distinct visual designs; some wield clubs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Customizable bike selection indicates player agency within racing community

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fourteen distinct opponent sprites; variable weapon equipment (clubs); differentiated motorcycle types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout five Californian tracks; positioned as competitors rather than faction members

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Police figures distinguished from racers; represent institutional opposition
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sporadic appearance on tracks; signals jurisdiction and penalty zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: California locations rendered with Grass Valley and Sierra Nevada environments; lower difficulty visual design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fewer aggressive opponents; reduced police enforcement frequency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Napa Valley and varied track designs; increased visual density of competitors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: More aggressive combat from opponents; increased stamina demands

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pacific Coast Highway environments; maximum track complexity and competitor density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Highest opponent aggression; peak motor officer enforcement; most demanding stamina management

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player character running on foot toward bike; visual speed reduction; exposed positioning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Inability to attack; susceptibility to incoming traffic; motor officer apprehension risk elevated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated punch, backhand, and kick actions; weapon transfer (club) when opponent disarmed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent stamina depletion visible in real-time; successful hits increment distance advantage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cash balance approaching zero or insufficient for repair/fine coverage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game termination forced; progression loss; return to password recovery necessary

environmental_storytelling.example: Five levels span California locations (Grass Valley → Sierra Nevada → Napa Valley → Pacific Coast Highway) suggesting journey across state; environment difficulty mirrors mechanical difficulty scaling
Violent combat mechanics justified by real Grand Prix motorcyclist behavior; unarmed and armed combat presented as normalized racing conduct rather than crime
Cash system communicates real-world racing costs; repair bills and fines are not abstract penalties but narrative economic pressures that can end career (game over)
Shop system between races allows player to visually transform motorcycle; purchase decisions reflect progression and investment in racing identity

environmental_storytelling.technique: Law enforcement presence as environmental opposition
environmental_storytelling.example: Motor officers appearing sporadically signal racing occurs within legal constraints; apprehension threat mirrors underground or illicit racing context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Punches, backhand strikes, kicks nearest opponent; stamina depletes with each action; weapon acquisition if opponent disarmed
Automatically runs toward bike; player can redirect trajectory or stand still; vulnerable to traffic; motor officer apprehension possible
Ejection from bike imminent; reduced offensive capability; heightened vulnerability
Extended reach attack capability; disarmable if struck; visual distinction indicates threat level
Sustained combat engagement; continuing to attack player; maintaining position in race
Ejection from bike; removal from race competition; visual confirmation of player combat success
Sporadic track appearance; enforces post-crash consequences; apprehends player if vulnerable
Damage meter decreases with crashes; visual or performance degradation; wreckage upon full depletion; repair cost incurred

notes: Road Rash communicates world-state primarily through mechanical meter visibility (stamina, bike damage, opponent stamina) rather than complex environmental narrative. Danger is signaled through immediate hazards (obstacles, opponents, police) and depleting resources (stamina, money, bike integrity). The game operates on a combat-racing hybrid model where violence is normalized as competition mechanic. Geographic setting (California locations) provides narrative scaffolding but minimal storytelling depth. Faction distinction is minimal; opposition is presented as economic (rival racers for prize money) and legal (police enforcement) rather than ideological or territorial. Visual communication prioritizes immediate gameplay state over world-building.
